blocks.text: Designed and shipped agentic LLM workflows that helped triage, research, and resolve customer support and data quality cases in a regulated healthcare environment. The system reduced manual handling, improved resolution speed, and maintained compliance guardrails through human review, evaluation criteria, and audit logging.
Problem
Support and operations teams manually researched repetitive data quality and customer issue cases.
Case context was fragmented across systems, slowing investigation and resolution.
Teams needed automation, but high-risk healthcare workflows required human review and auditability.
Without structured workflows, resolution quality varied by case complexity and operator experience.
Manual handling created unnecessary cost and slowed customer response times.
My Role
Owned product strategy, workflow design, evaluation criteria, risk guardrails, and launch execution as Senior Product Manager.
Partnered with Engineering, Security, Customer Success, Support, and clinical operations to define automation eligibility and quality requirements.
Defined which case types were safe to automate, where human review was required, and how quality would be measured before broader rollout.
Product Approach
Prioritized repetitive, high-volume case types with clear resolution patterns before expanding to ambiguous workflows.
Defined automation eligibility rules based on case risk, available context, and confidence thresholds.
Built human-in-the-loop checkpoints before scaling to reduce compliance and quality risk.
Defined quality thresholds before rollout to prevent automation from creating downstream support or compliance issues.
Solution

items.title: Case triage and classification
items.description: Automated categorization of incoming cases by type, risk level, and available context.

items.title: Context retrieval
items.description: Pulled relevant data from internal knowledge bases and support systems to inform each resolution.

items.title: LLM-generated recommendations
items.description: AI-drafted resolution suggestions and recommended next actions surfaced per case.

items.title: Human-in-the-loop review
items.description: Mandatory review checkpoints for ambiguous, sensitive, or low-confidence cases.

items.title: Escalation routing
items.description: Automatic escalation for cases below confidence thresholds or missing required context.

items.title: Audit logging
items.description: Full traceability of automated recommendations and actions for healthcare compliance review.

blocks.text: Workflow Design

blocks.title: Human-in-the-Loop Decision Flow
after: Missing context or no clear pattern
blocks.note: Before column → automated draft/resolution. After column → human review queue or escalation.

blocks.title: Input / Workflow / Output
steps: Input: support or data quality case

blocks.text: Tradeoffs & Product Decisions

blocks.tradeoff: Automated low-risk, repetitive cases first; routed ambiguous or sensitive cases to human review.
blocks.outcome: Meaningful automation gains without exposing regulated workflows to unreviewed AI output.

blocks.tradeoff: Optimized for resolution speed only after defining quality thresholds and review checkpoints.
blocks.outcome: Teams trusted the system enough to adopt it in production operations.

blocks.tradeoff: LLMs assisted triage and resolution, but every automated action was logged and reviewable.
blocks.outcome: Maintained the traceability required for healthcare compliance.

blocks.tradeoff: Focused MVP on case types with strong resolution patterns and available context.
blocks.outcome: High acceptance rate on automated suggestions in the initial rollout.

blocks.text: Results & Impact
Automated 2,000+ support and data quality cases through AI-assisted workflows.
Reduced manual effort by approximately $220K annually.
Improved resolution speed by reducing repetitive case research and manual triage.
Maintained traceability required for healthcare compliance through audit logging.
Created a reusable workflow foundation for future AI-assisted operations use cases.
Reduced operational burden while preserving human oversight for high-risk cases.
Metrics Tracked
Number of cases automated
Manual effort reduced
Annual cost savings
Human review / escalation rate
AI recommendation acceptance rate
Time to resolution
Audit traceability for automated actions
Case types eligible for automation
Key Learnings
AI workflow automation is most effective when focused on repetitive, high-volume workflows with clear resolution patterns.
In regulated environments, the product challenge is not just automation — it is designing the right balance of autonomy, human review, and traceability.
Human-in-the-loop systems build trust because teams can review, override, and improve AI-generated recommendations.
Evaluation criteria and escalation paths are essential before scaling LLM workflows into production operations.
